title: kubectx - (dash) fails silently when previous context file is empty string
body: swapContext() in cmd/kubectx/switch.go reads prev from readLastContext and returns errors.New("no previous context found") when prev == "", but if the state file exists and contains only whitespace/newline (e.g. from a partial write), prev is a non-empty string of whitespace and switchContext(prev) proceeds to fail deep inside kc.ContextExists with a confusing 'no context exists with the name' error instead of the clearer previous-context message.

title: Readonly proxy's nonMutatingPostPatterns list is missing selfsubjectaccessreviews.authorization.k8s.io v1beta1 variant
body: internal/proxy/readonly.go anchors nonMutatingPostPatterns to `/apis/authorization.k8s.io/[^/]+/...` which should match any version segment, but the review endpoints under other API groups (e.g. custom aggregated APIs that proxy authorization) aren't covered, so `kubectx --readonly` blocks some legitimate read-only POST requests used by kubectl auth can-i against those aggregated servers.

title: kubens statefile and kubectx prevCtxFile can get out of sync across concurrent shells
body: Both cmd/kubectx/state.go and cmd/kubens/statefile.go write their 'previous' pointer files independently with no locking, so running kubectx and kubens rapidly in two terminals against the same kubeconfig can race and leave one tool's back-and-forth (-) target pointing at a stale context/namespace.

title: fzf integration doesn't fall back gracefully when fzf exits non-zero from Ctrl-C
body: cmd/kubectx/fzf.go and cmd/kubens/fzf.go shell out to fzf for interactive selection; pressing Ctrl-C inside fzf returns a non-zero exit code that seems to bubble up as a generic 'failed to switch context' error rather than a clean no-op exit, which is inconsistent with just running kubectx with no args and pressing q.

title: Document COLOR/no-color behavior in internal/printer
body: internal/printer/color.go and printer.go control success/error coloring but README doesn't mention how NO_COLOR or a non-tty stderr affects output, which matters for users piping kubectx output into scripts.
